topic: EXP-A5 palette restriction proposal review (Phase A design refresh, queue_id 216)
our_argument_summary: EXP-210 (EXP-A5) is a draft design-refresh expansion that re-routes the legacy --refuted / --refuted-solid verdict tokens through EXP-202's --semantic-crit / --semantic-crit-soft tokens, neutralizes seven stat-card and entry-card border-left red accents to var(--rule), tokenizes the kill-shot pill-badges in part5.html, migrates the .ca-tag.tag-monitoring-hardcoded selector to amber/--semantic-warn, and neutralizes one [SUSPENDED] inline span to --ink-3. The proposal is HARD HOLD — DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW — and explicitly defers pie-chart SVG migration to Phase B and flags the kill-shot badge judgment call to the operator group.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Not applicable — design-refresh EXP. The relevant question is: do the listed source-file substitutions, when applied, satisfy the stated acceptance criteria and preserve all in-table verdict reds?
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: All affected literals are static CSS hex values in generate-html.js and sections.json. No computed paths involved.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: EXP is gated behind operator HNOTE; integration_blocked_until requires HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN naming EXP-210. Also requires EXP-202 to land first.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Two factual misses: (a) generate-html.js line 250 contains border-left:4px solid #8b0000 (Silent Failures sc-card in Part3 mini-summary) which is NOT included in the patch list — only the sibling line-245 #c45050 card is patched. The EXP claims #8b0000 lives only in part1.html sections.json. (b) The notes-for-human-review-group describes the pie chart as pre-rendered SVG requiring an offline emitter, but the pie chart is in fact generated at build time inside generate-html.js (lines 308-324 hold the lightColor/darkColor maps; lines 353+ emit the SVG with fill attributes). The work is not pre-rendered.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: EXP-210 is a CSS/design refactor; no monitoring axis applies.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Not a dome-claim review; design-refresh EXP.

holes_found.description: EXP-210 misses the second #8b0000 occurrence in build-scripts/generate-html.js. The change_2 patch list enumerates four generate-html.js locations (all #c45050) plus three sections.json locations (one of which is the part1.html Silent-Failures #8b0000). However, generate-html.js line 250 also contains: <div class="sc-card sc-sm" style="border-left:4px solid #8b0000"> — the Silent-Failures card in the Part3 "Failures the dome doesn't count" mini-summary header. This card sits directly next to the line-245 Acknowledged card that IS in the patch list, so the omission is structurally adjacent and easy to miss. Consequence: after integration, acceptance criterion [2] ("grep -c '#8b0000' docs/index.html returns 0") will FAIL, and the Silent-Failures mini-card will retain its saturated dark red border-left while the surrounding cards go neutral — producing an obvious visual asymmetry. The EXP's scope_item language ("neutralizes four stat-card border-left accents") undercounts by one.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a fifth generate-html.js patch entry for line 250: from `border-left:4px solid #8b0000` to `border-left:4px solid var(--rule)`. Update scope_item from "four stat-card border-left accents" to "five". Update change_2_stat_card_border_neutralization.where from "Two files, seven locations total" to "Two files, eight locations total."

holes_found.description: Anticipated objection #1 mischaracterizes the light-theme color shift as roughly JND. The objection only quotes the dark-theme delta (current --refuted-solid #ef5350 -> new --semantic-crit #d46a6a). In LIGHT theme — and therefore PRINT — the shift is far larger: --refuted-solid moves from #E57373 (HSL ~L=68%, light coral) to --semantic-crit #7a2e2e (HSL ~L=33%, dark burgundy). That is a ~35-point lightness drop, well beyond JND for any viewer, not just trained eyes. --refuted-solid is consumed by .vl-refuted, .vt-refuted (border-left-color), .ks-status.ks-claimed (text color), and the sc-card at line 1286 — all four selectors will visibly darken in light/print pathways. The note about light-theme delta in change_1 only addresses the --refuted background (the rgba(...,0.25) -> #f2dada migration, which is genuinely small) and silently skips the --refuted-solid solid-color migration. The print-pathway objection #5 dismisses regression with "the print PDF renders td.v-refuted cells in the same warm muted pink" — that is true for the cell background but NOT for the border-left or .ks-status text color, both of which now print as dark burgundy.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) rewrite anticipated objection #1 to honestly disclose the light-theme --refuted-solid shift and justify it on a11y / contrast-improvement grounds (the new color has better text contrast on white backgrounds, so this is defensible — just say so), or (b) split the migration: keep --refuted-solid at its current values and only re-route --refuted (background). The EXP itself proposes (b) as a fallback — promote it to the primary path or explicitly choose (a) with disclosure. Also extend the print-pathway test in objection #5 to verify the border-left and ks-status text colors, not just td backgrounds.

holes_found.description: Patch 1 in change_3_kill_shot_badge_migration is mislabeled and miscounted. The patch's `context` says "Kill shot 1 heading — Refuted by Data badge" with `occurrences_in_file: 1`. In reality, the substring `background:#FFCCCC;color:#B71C1C;padding:2px 8px;border-radius:3px;font-weight:600;">Refuted by Data` appears TWICE in part5.html, and BOTH matches are inside <td> cells of the Kill-Shot Verdict Summary table (rows for Test 6 NMP Drift Rate and Test 7 Stellar Parallax) — neither is in an <h2> heading. The actual h2-heading badges are the "Our Verdict: Refuted by Data" variants with margin-left:8px, which Patch 3 already covers correctly (count=2 matches reality). The functional impact is small: a global substring replace still migrates both td-cell badges, and migrating them to var(--semantic-crit) keeps them red — which is in fact consistent with the brief's strict rule (these ARE table cells flagging a contradiction). But the count and context label are wrong, and a verifier that uses occurrences_in_file as a sanity check will flag a mismatch.
holes_found.recommendation: Update Patch 1: set `context` to "Kill-Shot Verdict Summary table — verdict-badge cells for Tests 6 and 7" and `occurrences_in_file` to 2. Note in rationale that these are <td> cells and the migration to var(--semantic-crit) is consistent with strict-reading of the brief's in-table-cells-only rule (they keep their red, just tokenized).

holes_found.description: The pie-chart deferral note overstates the work required. notes_for_human_review_group says the pie chart "contains raw red hex literals (#E57373, #ef5350, #B71C1C) baked into pre-rendered SVG" and that "re-rendering it requires touching the pie-chart emitter function and adds complexity this EXP is trying to avoid." In reality the pie chart is generated at build time by generate-html.js: lines 311-324 declare a lightColor/darkColor map of hex strings keyed by verdict name, and lines 353+ emit the SVG path with fill="${lightColor}" attributes. There is no offline pre-render step — every build regenerates the SVG. Migrating the fills to CSS-var-driven values is a small edit (change the map values to CSS vars, switch fill= attribute to inline style="fill:var(...)"). The mapping decision (six verdict colors -> four semantic tokens) is non-trivial and is legitimately Phase B scope — but the technical complexity claim is inflated and could mislead the human review group into deferring a fix that is cheaper than described. This pairs with acceptance criterion [12] ("red pixels under 5% of total colored pixels") — with the pie chart unchanged, that target may be unmeetable, which means either A5 ships without satisfying its own success criterion or the pie chart must be touched in A5 anyway.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite the pie-chart deferral note to (a) accurately describe the chart as build-time-generated from generate-html.js color maps, not pre-rendered, and (b) acknowledge that the deferred work is the verdict-color-to-semantic-token MAPPING decision (which has no clean 1:1 from 6 verdicts to 4 tokens), not the technical SVG re-render. Either re-scope acceptance criterion [12] to exclude the pie chart explicitly, or commit to a pie-chart fix in A5 (or A5b) so the criterion is meetable.

holes_found.description: Test-coverage gap on token-chain resolution. Acceptance criterion [9] adds two assertions: (a) raw red hex count = 0 outside the pie SVG, and (b) --refuted/--refuted-solid reference --semantic-crit-soft/--semantic-crit. Both checks parse the source string. Neither asserts that td.v-refuted, .vt-refuted, .vl-refuted, .ks-status.ks-claimed actually RENDER with a non-default color in a built page. A typo in the EXP-202 token (e.g., --semantic-crit-sof) would silently produce colorless verdict cells and still pass both source-grep assertions. The site already runs Playwright for PDF generation — a screenshot-based or computed-style check is feasible.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a third acceptance check (or a follow-up EXP-A8 deliverable) that opens docs/index.html in headless Chromium and asserts the computed background-color and border-left-color of a sample td.v-refuted element are non-empty and within the expected red gamut.

holes_found.description: The judgment call on kill-shot <h2> badges (keep red via --semantic-crit) is correctly flagged to the human review group, but the rationale ("each kill-shot is a row in the argumentative sense") stretches the brief's plain language. The brief literally says "table cells" — kill-shot <h2> headings are not table cells. The analyst's argument is defensible but the human group should be told the strict-reading interpretation more directly. The current notes_for_human_review_group entry for this hedges on "each kill-shot is a row"; a cleaner framing would explicitly state: "Strict reading: neutralize. Analyst preference: keep red because the badge IS a verdict marker. Cost of flipping post-decision: trivial via token swap."
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten the kill-shot judgment-call note to lead with the strict reading, acknowledge it is the literal interpretation, and present the keep-red argument as an explicit deviation the operator group can accept or reject.

holes_found.description: Acceptance criterion [3] and [4] explicitly exempt the pie-chart SVG from the hex-literal grep, but criterion [12] asserts "red pixels under 5% of total colored pixels" without any pie-chart exemption. The criteria are internally inconsistent. With the pie chart left as a large red surface, [12] may not be measurable as passing.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a parenthetical to criterion [12]: "(measured excluding the verdict-distribution pie chart, which is data-viz; will be re-evaluated in Phase B once the pie is migrated)". Or, alternatively, drop criterion [12] from this EXP since the pie chart is out of scope and the criterion cannot be honestly evaluated without it.

stronger_arguments.description: The EXP's framing should explicitly note that the light-theme --refuted-solid shift (#E57373 -> #7a2e2e) is a Phase A WIN for accessibility — the new color has dramatically better contrast against white backgrounds (~7:1 vs the current ~2.5:1 for #E57373 text on white). Currently the EXP treats this shift as a potential negative to apologize for; the better framing is "the migration coincidentally fixes a long-standing a11y weakness in the light-theme verdict color."
sources: WCAG 2.1 contrast ratios for #E57373 vs #7a2e2e on #ffffff backgrounds — verifiable via any contrast checker.

stronger_arguments.description: The argument for keeping the FAIL-NNN entry-card border-lefts neutral could be reinforced by pointing out that those cards already carry visual failure markers in the FAIL-NNN id naming, the heading text, and the entry structure. The chrome-red was redundant signaling, not a load-bearing affordance. This makes the neutralization defensible against the objection "silent failures need to look like failures".
sources: Self-evident from inspection of the FAIL-NNN entry-card content in generate-html.js helper at line 214.

stronger_arguments.description: If the human review group accepts EXP-210, consider also emitting a build-time lint that fails the build on any new raw red hex outside the pie SVG block. The EXP's test.js assertions catch the current snapshot but not future regressions; a CI lint hook is a sharper guard. The notes_for_human_review_group already flags this as STRICT-RED TEST POSSIBILITY — promoting it from a flag to an actual EXP-A5b deliverable is cheap.
sources: EXP-210 notes_for_human_review_group entry on STRICT-RED TEST POSSIBILITY.

kernel_of_truth.description: The core thesis of EXP-210 — that the site's ambient red chrome (stat-card border-lefts, FAIL-NNN entry cards, [SUSPENDED] prose ink) signals "refuted" before the reader has read any evidence, while the actual verdict pills inside tables earn their red — is correct and well-aligned with the brief's Item 7. The token-rerouting strategy (re-route --refuted/--refuted-solid through EXP-202's semantic tokens rather than touching every consumer selector) is clean, preserves backward compatibility, and pre-stages the kill-shot badge flip as a one-line swap. Escalating the kill-shot badge judgment call and the pie-chart deferral to the human review group is good process. The .ca-tag.tag-monitoring-hardcoded reclassification to amber is semantically correct (warning, not refutation).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The strategy is sound but the execution has factual gaps that need to be patched before integration. Specifically: a missed #8b0000 occurrence breaks the EXP's own acceptance criterion, and the light-theme color-shift mischaracterization could lead the human review group to approve under a false-JND premise and then notice a dramatic light-mode visual change post-integration. These are correctable in a draft revision; they do not invalidate the design.

deeper_analysis:
EXP-210 is a well-structured proposal that correctly identifies redundant red chrome in three categories (stat-card borders, FAIL-NNN entry cards, prose-inline tags) and migrates them to neutral or warn tokens while preserving in-table verdict reds via token rerouting. The token-rerouting approach is the right architectural choice — it fixes the palette globally without churning every selector that consumes --refuted, and it sets up Phase A8 (a11y glyph pairing) as a clean follow-up. The proposal's explicit gating on operator HNOTE and EXP-202 ordering is correct.

The two material gaps — a missed source location (line 250 #8b0000) and a mischaracterized visual delta in the light theme — are both verifiable by direct inspection of the source files. The decider should NOT integrate EXP-210 in its current form; the analyst should produce a v2 that adds the missing patch entry, corrects Patch 1's context/count, rewrites the JND objection to honestly disclose the light-theme shift, and (optionally) re-evaluates whether the pie-chart deferral note overstates complexity.

The judgment call on kill-shot <h2> badges is genuinely a judgment call and is correctly escalated — the curmudgeon has no strong opinion either way, but the operator group should hear the strict reading clearly stated. The pie-chart deferral is reasonable on scope grounds (mapping 6 colors to 4 tokens is not trivial) but the technical-complexity argument is inflated.

Other side of the argument: a defender of EXP-210 as-drafted could reasonably say (a) the line-250 miss is a typo-class error fixable in a one-line patch revision, not a design flaw; (b) the light-theme shift may be intentional and welcome as an a11y improvement, just under-disclosed; (c) the pie-chart deferral is correct on scope even if the complexity description is slightly off; (d) the kill-shot badge judgment call is being properly handled via escalation, not unilateral decision. None of these defenses change the recommendation — fix the gaps before integration — but they bound the criticism: this is a draft that needs minor surgery, not a structurally flawed proposal.

citations_verified: Lines 214, 245, 1255, 1532 in build-scripts/generate-html.js contain the four #c45050 border-left declarations as claimed.
Line 484 in build-scripts/generate-html.js contains the .ca-tag.tag-monitoring-hardcoded selector with #FFEBEE/#B71C1C/#EF9A9A as claimed.
Line 203 in build-scripts/generate-html.js contains the [SUSPENDED] inline span with color:#8b0000 as claimed (one occurrence).

EXP-202.json defines --semantic-crit (#7a2e2e light / #d46a6a dark), --semantic-crit-soft (#f2dada / #3d1f1f), --semantic-warn (#8a5a1e / #d99860), --semantic-warn-soft (#f3e6d3 / #3d2e20), --rule (#E4DDCB / #2e2e4a), --ink-3 (#6E675B / #8a8680) as referenced.
Three #FFCDD2 occurrences in data/sections.json correspond to three ks-rating-decisive Decisive badges as claimed.
Two #FFCCCC occurrences in data/sections.json correspond to verdict-badges with margin-left:8px starting with "Our Verdict: Refuted by Data" (Patch 3 count of 2 is correct).
citations_failed: EXP-210 claim that #8b0000 lives only in part1.html sections.json — generate-html.js line 250 also has it.
EXP-210 Patch 1 occurrences_in_file: 1 — actual count is 2 (both in <td> cells of the Kill-Shot Verdict Summary table, not in <h2> headings as the context label suggests).
EXP-210 notes-for-human-review-group description of the pie chart as "pre-rendered SVG" — pie chart is built at request time inside generate-html.js (lines 311-324 colors, 353+ emission).
citations_unchecked: Approximate line 1286 sc-card border-left:4px solid var(--refuted-solid) — verified in grep as line 1286 reference; consumer-side impact of the token rerouting was reasoned about but not visually rendered.
Test.js current assertions — not enumerated to confirm the two new ones do not collide with existing assertions; recommendation is to add but the EXP says "unchanged" for existing.
Pie chart slice fill JS at lines 311-324 was inspected for color literals; full pie-render path was not end-to-end traced.

notes_for_decider: Recommend HOLDING integration of EXP-210 in its current form pending an analyst v2 revision that addresses holes #1 and #2 (missing #8b0000 patch and light-theme JND mischaracterization) at minimum. Holes #3, #4, #5 are nice-to-fix but not blockers. The hard hold on operator HNOTE remains regardless. Suggest the decider write a HOLD note attached to EXP-210 in monitor/decisions/open-issues.json (or equivalent) so the analyst sees the curmudgeon findings before the operator group reviews the proposal — otherwise the human group may approve a draft with known correctness gaps.
